page: Amazon Web Services i3en.metal is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors. It features 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture with a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Storage is a key highlight of this instance, featuring 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and Passmark database operations all ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with read-only large block speeds reaching 224.36 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 97.66 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core tests, falls into the average category. The server excels in high-throughput database operations, demonstrated by a strong Redis SET performance of 77.09 million operations per second and static web serving throughput of 50.77 GB/sec. However, LLM text generation speeds are weak to poor due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i3en.metal offers a high density of local NVMe storage and memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads that require massive local storage capacity without the overhead of virtualization. The server is optimized for I/O-intensive workloads, including large-scale relational and non-relational databases, distributed file systems, data warehousing, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i3en.metal is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server designed for high-throughput, I/O-intensive workloads. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor offering 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) at 3.1 GHz, 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, the server delivers 100 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database operation speeds, including 77.09 million Redis SET operations per second. Single-core performance and LLM text generation are average to weak. This server is highly suited for large-scale databases, distributed file systems, and data warehousing applications requiring massive local storage and high network throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, 60 TB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.metal is a storage-optimized bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 60 TB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A bare-metal server delivering sixty terabytes of local NVMe storage and high-bandwidth networking for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors
60,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
768.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Strong multi-core CPU and database performance, including Redis and Passmark benchmarks
Optimized for large-scale databases, distributed file systems, and data warehousing
